the Sun and the Moon and divide it by two. If the result agrees
with the longitude of the ascendant, descendant, M.C. or I.C.,
then we have the correct time of birth. In this manner, we
may represent the following valid equations:-

Directions: Some Attempted Equations 109
1. (a) (Long. of Sun+ Long. of Moon) divided by 2; (b) (2 X
Long. of Ascdt.) +Long. of Moon divided by 3; (c) Long. of Sun
+ (2 X Long. of Moon) divided by 3;: Ascendant, Descendant,
M.C. or I.C .

X Long. of Ascdt.) +Long. of Moon divided by 3; (c) Long. of
Ascdt. + (2 X long. of Moon) divided by 3 ·= Sun, M.C. or I. C. ;

long. ofM.C.) +Long. of Sun divided by 3; (c) M.C. + (2 X Long.
of Sun) divided by 3 =Moon, Ascdt. or Descdt.
